article_title: Bezos-Backed Slate Auto Raises $650 Million Series C to Bring Its Mid-$20K Electric Truck to Production by Year-End

content_preview.title: Bezos-Backed Slate Auto Raises $650 Million Series C to Bring Its Mid-$20K Electric Truck to Production by Year-End
content_preview.summary: Slate Auto closes a $650 million Series C led by TWG Global, bringing total funding to roughly $1.4 billion as the startup prepares to manufacture affordable electric trucks at a converted Indiana factory.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Slate Auto, the electric vehicle startup backed by Jeff Bezos, has closed a $650 million Series C funding round as it races to begin manufacturing affordable electric trucks before the end of 2026. The round was led by existing investor TWG Global, the firm run by Los Angeles Dodgers owner Mark Walter, and brings the company's total capital raised to approximately $1.4 billion, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/13/slate-auto-raises-650m-to-fund-its-affordable-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong EV business article on Slate Auto Series C funding round.
editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch VERIFIED via WebFetch: $650M Series C, TWG Global lead, $1.4B total, Bezos-backed, mid-$20K pricing, 160K reservations, Indiana factory, end-2026 production all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial and operational details verified against TechCrunch primary source.
